perturbation.original_text: Following written notice by one party to another of the termination of discussions between the parties, upon written request of the disclosing party, the receiving party shall take reasonable steps to instruct all persons involved in the Transaction to destroy all Confidential Information furnished to the receiving party by or on behalf of the disclosing party pursuant to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Following written notice by one party to another of the termination of discussions between the parties, upon written request of the disclosing party, the receiving party may choose, at its sole discretion, to destroy all Confidential Information furnished to the receiving party by or on behalf of the disclosing party pursuant to this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change makes the destruction of confidential information discretionary ('may choose') instead of mandatory ('shall take reasonable steps'). This creates a direct in-text contradiction with Section 1, where the receiving party agrees to take measures to protect the confidentiality of the information. Making the destruction optional undermines this commitment and creates uncertainty about the receiving party's obligations after the termination of discussions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party agrees that it will take commercially reasonable measures to protect the confidentiality of, and to avoid having Confidential Information of the other party enter the public domain, become publicly available, or be held by persons not authorized hereunder to have such Confidential Information, which measures shall include at least the same degree of care that the receiving party utilizes to protect its own Confidential Information of a similar nature. Each party agrees to notify the other party in writing of any misuse or misappropriation of such Confidential Information of the other party that may come to its attention.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original text by making the destruction of confidential information discretionary ('may choose') instead of mandatory ('shall take reasonable steps'). This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that each party agrees to take commercially reasonable measures to protect the confidentiality of information. Making the destruction optional undermines this commitment, creating a strong contradiction and uncertainty about the receiving party's obligations, thus violating the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: To the fullest extent permitted by law, each of the parties hereby agrees to waive trial by jury in any action proceeding or counterclaim brought by or on behalf of either party with respect to any matter whatsoever relating to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: To the fullest extent permitted by law, only the Company hereby agrees to waive trial by jury in any action proceeding or counterclaim brought by or on behalf of either party with respect to any matter whatsoever relating to this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By limiting the jury trial waiver to 'only the Company', this change creates an in-text contradiction. While the original agreement intends both parties to waive this right, the altered text implies that HPE retains the right to a jury trial. This is contradicted by Section 14's initial statement that 'each of the parties hereby agrees and irrevocably consents to personal jurisdiction and venue', making the allocation of legal rights ambiguous and potentially leading to disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Governing Law and Jurisdiction . This Agreement shall be governed by and construed and enforced in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware applicable to agreements made and to be performed within that state. Each of the parties hereby agrees and irrevocably consents to personal jurisdiction and venue in any federal or state court within Wilmington, Delaware, having subject matter jurisdiction, for the purposes of any action, suit or proceeding arising out of or relating to this agreement. To the fullest extent permitted by law, only the Company hereby agrees to waive trial by jury in any action proceeding or counterclaim brought by or on behalf of either party with respect to any matter whatsoever relating to this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text limits the jury trial waiver to 'only the Company', while the original text applied the waiver to 'each of the parties'. This creates a direct conflict because the contradicted text, which includes the changed text, now contains conflicting statements about which parties waive their right to a jury trial. Therefore, the altered text introduces a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement among the parties hereto and supersedes all other prior agreements and understandings, both written and oral, among or between any of the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement among the parties hereto with the exception of previously signed agreements pertaining to intellectual property and supersedes all other prior agreements and understandings, both written and oral, among or between any of the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and thereof.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the phrase 'with the exception of previously signed agreements pertaining to intellectual property', the modified text creates an exception to the entire agreement clause. This directly contradicts the main claim that *all* prior agreements are superseded. The vagueness of 'previously signed agreements pertaining to intellectual property' introduces uncertainty, since Section 4 (No License Granted) makes no such exception, raising questions on intellectual property rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No License Granted . Nothing in this Agreement is intended to grant any rights to either party under any patent, copyright, trade secret or other intellectual property right, nor shall this Agreement grant either party any rights in or to the other party’s Confidential Information, except the limited right to review such Confidential Information solely for the purposes of determining whether to enter into, and the undertaking of or the advising with respect to, a Transaction. 5. No Representation of Accuracy .· Each party acknowledges that neither the other party nor any of its Associates has made any representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Confidential Information made available by the other party or its Associates. Each party agrees that, except as set forth in a definitive agreement executed by the parties, neither party nor any of its Associates shall have any liability to the other party or to any of its Associates relating to or resulting from the use of such other party’s Confidential Information or any errors therein m omissions therefrom. Only those representations or warranties made in a definitive agreement, when, as and if executed, and subject to such limitations and restrictions as may be specified therein, will have any legal effect.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement supersedes all prior agreements. The changed text introduces an exception for previously signed agreements pertaining to intellectual property. This directly contradicts the original statement, because the agreement no longer supersedes *all* prior agreements. This contradiction is further emphasized by the fact that the Contradicted Text (No License Granted) makes no such exception, raising questions on intellectual property rights, therefore making the terms directly conflicting.

perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees that it will take commercially reasonable measures to protect the confidentiality of, and to avoid having Confidential Information of the other party enter the public domain, become publicly available, or be held by persons not authorized hereunder to have such Confidential Information, which measures shall include at least the same degree of care that the receiving party utilizes to protect its own Confidential Information of a similar nature.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party agrees that it will take measures it deems necessary to protect the confidentiality of, and to avoid having Confidential Information of the other party enter the public domain, become publicly available, or be held by persons not authorized hereunder to have such Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the 'commercially reasonable' standard and the clause about applying 'the same degree of care that the receiving party utilizes to protect its own Confidential Information of a similar nature' introduces an in-text contradiction. Section 1 already says the receiving party 'shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by its Associates' but now, the parties have greater discretion on the measures deemed necessary, making it more ambiguous and harder to determine if they adequately protected the data.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each of the parties hereto agree not to use the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the other party or its Associates except in connection with preparing a proposal for and any discussions of a Transaction or as otherwise permitted hereunder. Neither party will disclose any Confidential Information of the other party to third parties except those of its Associates who are required to have the information in order to evaluate and discuss a Transaction. The receiving party shall inform its Associates of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and shall instruct them to treat the information confidentially. The receiving party shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by its Associates and agrees, at its sole expense, to take all commercially reasonable measures to restrain its Associates from disclosure or use of the Confidential Information in breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the 'commercially reasonable measures' and 'same degree of care' standards for protecting confidential information. The contradicted text states 'The receiving party shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by its Associates and agrees, at its sole expense, to take all commercially reasonable measures to restrain its Associates from disclosure or use of the Confidential Information in breach of this Agreement.' By removing the 'commercially reasonable' standard in the changed text, it creates a contradiction with the contradicted text, making it unclear what level of measures the receiving party is required to take, and whether they are in compliance.
